name: Aimeos PHP E-Commerce Framework API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP e-commerce framework with REST API — enabling PHP developers to build high-performance online stores using Laravel, Symfony, or TYPO3, with support for multi-vendor/marketplace, subscriptions, digital goods, B2B, and multi-site deployments, with a built-in REST API for headless commerce and mobile app backends.
use_cases: Building Laravel e-commerce store via Aimeos REST API for headless commerce architecture
Creating multi-vendor marketplace via Aimeos for platform business models
Building B2B order management system via Aimeos for wholesale/trade customer portals
Implementing subscription commerce via Aimeos for recurring revenue digital goods
Integrating Aimeos with custom frontend via REST API for decoupled commerce stack
not_for: Non-PHP backends (use Saleor for Python/Django, vendure for Node.js/GraphQL)
Teams without PHP/Laravel/Symfony expertise for setup and customization
Small teams needing hosted SaaS without server management (use Shopify)
best_when: A PHP development team needs a highly customizable, enterprise-grade open-source e-commerce framework — where Aimeos provides full Laravel/Symfony integration, multi-vendor support, and a REST API for headless commerce without per-transaction fees.
avoid_when: You're not using PHP, need a hosted SaaS without PHP server management, or need a consumer-facing marketplace with network effects.

auth.notes: REST API auth configurable via Laravel/Symfony auth middleware. API key (token) auth or Laravel Sanctum token auth. OAuth2 via Laravel Passport integration. No built-in webhook system. Self-hosted — auth configured per deployment. Multi-vendor: separate vendor tokens. Admin API requires admin authentication (separate from storefront API).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (LGPL v3) — completely free self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: Professional extensions from €199 (one-time); Enterprise license ~€3,000/year (additional features)
pricing.notes: German company (Aimeos GmbH, Nuremberg). Founded 2010. LGPL v3 (core) with commercial extensions. GitHub: aimeos/aimeos-core (3,600+ stars). PHP 8.x+. Performance focus: benchmark claims 20,000 requests/min. Supports: Laravel, Symfony, TYPO3. Multi-tenant, multi-site, multi-vendor. Subscription support (recurring billing). Digital goods. B2B features: customer groups, tiered pricing. REST and GraphQL in newer versions. SEO-optimized. Used by: German SMBs, agencies. Fewer US users than Sylius/Saleor. Professional support contracts available. Aimeos TYPO3 integration popular in Germany/Europe.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no limits; server capacity dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API via Laravel/Symfony. Auth configurable per deployment. LGPL open source. Self-hosted PHP. No webhooks out-of-box. Performance-focused (20K req/min claim). German/European community. Smaller English docs ecosystem vs Sylius/Saleor.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product/category reads idempotent. Order creation: check for existing orders before creating. Laravel/Symfony ORM handles underlying idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted PHP — REST API URL structure depends on Laravel/Symfony routing configuration
Auth method depends on deployment — Sanctum token, Passport OAuth2, or custom — check deployment
No built-in webhook system — use Laravel events/listeners for event-driven integrations
German-origin project — documentation more complete in German community resources
LGPL v3 core with commercial extensions — some features require paid license
Multi-vendor vs single-store setups have different API paths and auth flows

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. LGPL open source — auditable. User-controlled data residency. Auth via Laravel/Symfony middleware (CSRF, token, OAuth2 all supported). No central webhook exposure. German company — GDPR by default in design. Customer PII management per deployment.
